Figure 1: Interface design for interactive route planning.

Figure 4: The seven possibly optimal routes in OsdorpMidden. The blue marker (above) indicates the origin and the red marker (below) the destination. Each colored path corresponds to one Pareto-undominated solution with objective vector [𝑑, 𝑐], where 𝑑 denotes route length (in metres) and 𝑐 the number of crossings. The yellow-green route on the extreme left corresponds to [928, 3], while the orange route extending furthest to the right corresponds to [1335, 2]. In the central region, the pink and brown routes in the upper part correspond to [703, 4] and [603, 5], respectively, and the red route in the lower central area corresponds to [586, 6]. The two routes closest to the lower boundary are the dark blue route [568, 8] and the light blue route [574, 7].
Figure 5: Average maximum utility for Osdorp-Midden PF with 7 solutions (noise=0.01)
